title: On immigration, Spain moves against the tide of its European neighbors
subhed: Citing economic and moral arguments, Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's Socialist government is preparing to grant legal status to 500,000 undocumented workers, most from Latin America. The plan brings hope to the 840,000 foreign nationals living without papers in Spain.

Greek PM calls for launch of fraud proceedings in EU agricultural subsidies case
subhed: European Union prosecutors are probing 'numerous' members of the Greek ruling party for fraud claims worth at least €23 million.

title: Key facts to understand cadmium, an emerging public health issue
subhed: Cadmium, a heavy metal toxic to human health, is found in many everyday foods. Children and women are most affected by this carcinogenic contaminant, which is the subject of growing concern.

title: Sex is an art form hidden in the shadows
subhed: Moral panic over the pleasures of the flesh prevents us from recognizing the artistic dimension of sexuality, as well as from passing down the best practices, argues Le Monde columnist  Maïa Mazaurette.

title: War denies Lebanon's dead a final resting place
subhed: Forced to flee southern Lebanon due to Israeli air strikes and evacuation orders from the Israeli army, thousands of Lebanese have resigned themselves to burying loved ones far from their ancestral lands in makeshift graves.

How 'The Camp of the Saints' became the far right's cult novel, from the Le Pens to MAGA
subhed: Since its publication in 1973, Jean Raspail's novel has shaped the imagination of ideologues who oppose immigration. The openly racist story, which envisions a million Indians descending on the French coast, was recently republished across the Atlantic.

title: 'Retirees are no happier than working people'
subhed: It is simplistic to equate happiness with retirement, as retirement often appeals to those seeking to escape increasingly difficult working conditions, observes Le Monde's Béatrice Madeline in her column, drawing on a study by two economists.

title: Le Duc brings the fresh taste of the sea to Paris
subhed: For nearly 60 years, Le Duc, a family-run Parisian institution known for its delicate fish recipes, has drawn a loyal following who come to savor impossibly thinly sliced, pearly raw sea bass or langoustines with red shiso and wasabi.
